title: Sub-step 3a: Update travelogue fetch logic to use journeyId

requirements.text: Update the travelogue data fetch logic so that travelogue entries are queried and loaded by journeyId rather than by city name or any global/shared key. This change must be confined strictly to the fetch/read path for travelogue data. No other UI layout, panel, component, or styling must be modified.

quotes: Sub-step 3a: Travelogue fetch logic only — updating the fetch/query to use journeyId, with no changes to any unrelated UI layouts, panels, or components
Update the function/hook that loads travelogue data so it queries by journeyId instead of by city name or a global key

acceptanceCriteria: The travelogue fetch function/hook accepts a journeyId parameter and uses it as the lookup key when calling the backend
Each journey's travelogue data is fetched independently — two journeys sharing the same city return their own separate travelogue entries
No other frontend components, panels, layouts, or styles are altered by this change
No backend data model fields are added or removed; only the query/lookup key used for fetching is changed
The travelogue save, delete, and clear paths remain untouched

constraints: Only the travelogue fetch/read logic may be changed — no modifications to save, delete, or clear paths
No changes to any unrelated UI layouts, panels, components, or styles
Do not alter backend data model fields or other query hooks outside the travelogue fetch path
nonGoals: Any UI layout, panel, or styling changes
Changes to non-travelogue data queries or hooks
